BASIC ATTACK: 
An attack with up to 5 sequences that deals <@ba.natur>Nature DMG</>. As the controlled operator, <#ba.lastcombo>Final Strike</> also deals <@ba.poise>{poise:0}</> Stagger. 

DIVE ATTACK: 
Basic attack performed in mid-air becomes a dive attack that deals <@ba.natur>Nature DMG</> to nearby enemies. 

FINISHER: 
Basic attack performed near a Staggered enemy becomes a finisher that deals massive <@ba.natur>Nature DMG</> and recovers some SP.
added.-4782919681473767098:
COMBO TRIGGER: When an <#ba.spellinflict>Arts Infliction</> is applied on an enemy.
Arcane charges at the target and sets up 4 <@ba.key>Tactical Doubles</> that deal <@ba.natur>Nature DMG</> and apply another stack of <#ba.spellinflict>Arts Infliction</> of the same element, <#ba.naturalvul>Nature Susceptibility</>, and <#ba.crystvul>Cryo Susceptibility</> to the target and nearby enemies, and also apply <@ba.key>Imprisonment</> on the said target. When <@ba.key>Imprisonment</> ends, the <@ba.key>Tactical Doubles</> will attack the said target to deal Explosion DMG.
<@ba.key>Imprisonment</>: A debuff that slows all actions.
<@ba.key>Array Arcana: WILL</>:
- Applying <@ba.key>Imprisonment</> by casting a combo skill will also slightly <@ba.key>pull in</> enemies near the target.
- The attribute of Will can further increase the effects of <#ba.naturalvul>Nature Susceptibility</> and <#ba.crystvul>Cryo Susceptibility</> applied by the combo skill.

<@intru.bold>- Stage Rating -</> 
<@gray.note>· Every stage has 3 difficulty tiers: Normal (rewards 1★ when completed), Hard (rewards 2★ when completed), and Brutal (rewards 3★ when completed).</> 
<@gray.note>· Completing a stage in Brutal difficulty and fulfilling the bonus challenge goals grants the [Lustrous] boost to the rating.</> 
<@gray.note>· The highest Total Rating that can be earned from a cycle (by successfully completing 3 stages in Brutal difficulty) is 9★.</> 
<@gray.note>· After a cycle ends, the Total Rating will be reset for the new cycle. Challenge the stages of the new cycle to earn stars again. Total Rating from past cycles will be recorded for determining the [Final Rating] of the current season.</> 

<@intru.bold>- Unlocking Stages -</> 
<@gray.note>· When a cycle begins, Normal and Hard difficulty tiers will be available by default for the stages. Complete a stage in Hard difficulty to unlock its Brutal difficulty.</> 
<@gray.note>· Earning the [Lustrous] title for the current season automatically unlocks Brutal difficulty for all stages in the next season.</>

Arcane's combo skill is triggered when an <#ba.spellinflict>Arts Infliction</> is applied on an enemy. The combo skill applies another <#ba.spellinflict>Arts Infliction</> of the same element, <#ba.naturalvul>Nature Susceptibility</>, and <#ba.crystvul>Cryo Susceptibility</> to the said enemy and targets nearby, and also applies the <@ba.key>Imprisonment</> debuff on the said enemy. 
An enemy under <@ba.key>Imprisonment</> will experience a slower flow of time. When <@ba.key>Imprisonment</> ends, Arcane's <@ba.key>Tactical Doubles</> will attack the said enemy to deal Explosion DMG.

added.-8626532795785343512: During Talosian Calendar Year 142, the Tianshi stationed at Wuling Science Station of the Hongshan Academy of Sciences (HAS) attempted to reverse the growth of the Very Large Rift (VLR) with Xiranite. This experiment triggered a rift rupture of disastrous effects, with Wuling Science Station and its prototype Xiranite facilities being completely engulfed by the Blight Tide within two hours. Although the HAS directed vast amounts of resources and personnel to support subsequent search-and-rescue operations, only a handful of survivors were saved. The HAS then committed all of its emergency response forces to contain the disaster within Yinglung Pass and enforced strict access control to the stricken area that covered the entirety of Wuling Science Station. This area became known as the North Wuling EZ.

<@intru.bold>- Participation Requirements -</>
<@gray.note>· Complete the main mission [Chapter Ⅰ Process Ⅱ: Remediation Progress].</>

<@intru.bold>- Event Rules -</>
<@gray.note>· This event screen lets you directly unlock and accept the main mission [Chapter Ⅱ Process Ⅰ: Arrival at Wuling] early.</>
<@gray.note>· After unlocking the Chapter Ⅱ main mission early, simply follow the mission guide and head to the region of Wuling.</>
<@gray.note>· Main mission progressions can be saved separately. Early unlock of main missions in subsequent chapters will not affect your original main mission progress.</>
<@gray.note>· Early unlock of main missions in subsequent chapters might affect the sequence and timing for unlocking various gameplay features and systems.</>
<@gray.note>· When experiencing the processes of early unlocked main missions, a batch of Early Unlock Facilities will be made available to help you set up your AIC Factory.</>
<@gray.note>· Main missions in subsequent chapters may involve characters and relationships that have not yet been revealed. Completing the Chapter Ⅰ main missions is still recommended for the best narrative experience.</>

added.-7998916740689663069:
The Captain and Chen? I don't know much about that. I heard they were pretty good friends and even partnered up during the selection trials. Their performance was also the most outstanding amongst the reservists. Everyone thought they would become a pair of promising Yinglungs garrisoned at Yinglung Pass. But after that ... they wouldn't stop arguing with each other. And if they weren't, they were preparing for the next shouting match. You know the Captain. She never backs down. The two were assigned to separate groups and the Captain was the one that became an Yinglung... What if Chen becomes an Yinglung...? I ... can't imagine that. The Captain will still be the Captain. And if she were ever assigned to the Captain's team ... someone would definitely get reassigned. As a bystander, Instructor Zhu and the Captain made the right call. The current arrangement is the best for both of them. 
— Summit, trooper, Yinglung Special Task Force 

The YSTF didn't teach the Captain how to wield her heavy arts unit cannon. I asked her once, and she said it's part of her father's "legacy". There was a time when the Captain couldn't get any better no matter how hard she trained, so she kept swapping her weapons. Blades, spears, swords, halberds, axes, broadaxes, hooks, and tridents... She tried them all, got decent with everything, mastered all sorts of fighting styles, but she wasn't satisfied. She thought she wasn't getting good enough, so she doubled her training regimen... People got worried that she'd collapse from exhaustion. Then one day she chanced upon her father's arts unit cannon that was stored outside the place. She never applied for leave until then. No one knew where she went for a couple of days. But when she came back, she had made up her mind to use her father's weapon. And after that, she just became the Arcane we know. No Yinglung garrisoned at Wuling is a match for her. 
— Phrase, trooper, Yinglung Special Task Force 

STOP LISTENING TO THE RUMORS! Ugh. During the selection trials, she ... welp, she was like ... yeah ... got that slight edge over me. But I'm a far better fighter than I used to! If we fight again, I'll give her three free attacks on me! Wait ... maybe two. Don't worry. It'd be just a friendly spar. From what I remember, Arcane was always frowning and grumpy, like she was constantly carrying a huge boulder around. But since she fulfilled her wish about the North Wuling EZ, she's turned a little different. Oh yeah! It reminds me of how she looked like when I first met her. I tried asking her about her encounters in the EZ but she refused to say anything to me. I can't just go and pester the Endmin with questions... Wait... I think I can... 
— Chen Qianyu, former reservist, Yinglung Special Task Force 

Li Zhiyan? That little Yinglung girl? Nope. Nothing special comes to mind. Every time I see her, she's always stern-faced, stiff-necked, and making people feel bad about themselves. And she wouldn't stop yapping "Lady Proxy! Lady Proxy!" and I had to correct her umpteen times before I could force her to change. Mortal life is short, yet she's blessed with powerful abilities. But look at what she's doing with them: Living the most boring life ever and shackling herself with a load of self-made rules. Well, things have gotten a bit better recently. Her face softened up a little, so that's quite interesting, at least. Maybe the silly dolt did something. Hmph. That clueless idiot romped around and ended up with a bunch of injuries. There'll be no escape this time. 
— Si

added.-7603239588492036433:
<@intru.bold>- Participation Requirements -</> 
<@gray.note>· Endministrator Authority Level ≥ 40</> 
<@gray.note>· Complete the main mission [Chapter Ⅰ Process Ⅱ: Paving the Way].</>

<@intru.bold>- Gameplay Details -</> 
<@gray.note>· [Echoes of War] is a seasonal game mode that is permanently available. Challenge various stages, earn stars, and achieve various season titles.</> 
<@gray.note>· Each season includes multiple cycles. Each cycle usually lasts no less than 7 days. On the last day of a cycle, the next cycle begins at 04:00 (server time) of the following day.</> 
<@gray.note>· Each cycle includes 3 stages. The total number of stars earned from the 3 stages represents the Total Rating of that cycle.</> 
<@gray.note>· After a cycle ends, the Total Rating will be reset for the new cycle. Challenge the stages of the new cycle to earn stars again. Total Rating from past cycles will be recorded for determining the [Final Rating] of the season.</> 
<@gray.note>· The highest Total Rating earned across all cycles in the current season will be the [Final Rating] of the season. The Final Rating also determines the season title you receive, Endministrator.</> 
<@gray.note>· The season title of the current season will be displayed at UI related to the Friends feature.</>
<@gray.note>· Check the [Echoes of War] event screen for more gameplay details.</>

added.-4974650165152922581:
Can you believe it, Lonna? I'm not on Talos-II when delivering this letter. 
You probably guessed it too. That's right! I was part of the special delegation from the Talos-II General Chamber of Commerce, and we were attending a closed-door meeting with Endfield aboard their ship, Dijiang. 
It was my first time boarding a vessel capable of traversing the sea of stars. And what a sea it is! It's far deeper and quieter than the oceans we have, with faint stars glittering in the distance. From space, Talos-II looks just like baby Baily fast asleep in his crib. 
Oh, my cute little Baily. Give our sweet baby a kiss on the head for me. 
I thought all my curiosities could be satisfied by the stars and the view of Talos-II from space, but on day four, Endfield arranged something special for us as part of the reception. We visited their zero gravity experience chamber. It looked like a simple, empty room. When we were ready, the lights grew dim and the sky window slowly opened, allowing the light from Talos the gas giant to pour in. The huge planet seized all of our attention. 
And then, gravity itself disappeared. 
Even for our negotiators and corporate officers. All of them lost their solemn gravity and instincts took over. My ears were filled with gasps of astonishment and wonder. I even heard a soft chuckle, and I swear it came from the Endfield operator who served as our guide. Looking at big-timer suits acting like playful or scared children must have offered a pure sense of joy. If this wasn't a closed-door and confidential meeting, the walls of this zero-g chamber would be plastered with photos of its "victims". 
As for me... I don't know how to say this to you, Lonna. When I floated in the sky, abandoned by every ounce of my weight and by the land itself, a refreshing sense of freedom coursed through my body as I faced the celestial object before me. I... 
I felt a sense of duty. 
A sense of the most impossible duty. 
I suddenly felt compelled to bring this experience to every resident of the Civilization Band. 
We are the children born into the cradle of Talosian gravity. But the birth of Dijiang would be the first time in our history when we finally have the chance to live differently. 
I remember father telling me that the most priceless thing is not a luxurious mansion or merchandise. That honor goes to the way of life. 
Can anything be more special than transcending the grips of gravity and living amongst the stars? 
We'll get an artificial geostationary orbit experience cabins in the future. We start from the basics, then work our way towards commercial complexes and space arenas. Every sport will get new rules, new tournaments, and new superstars competing in the zero-g environment. When knights wearing jet packs rocket through arenas, when race cars make perfect turns across toroid tracks, when the football flies into the goal under the influence of mechanics and gravity... When all these happen, our achievements will far outstrip any sum of money we imagined, Lonna. 
Of course, none of this would be possible without your support, dear. Do remember to take a look at the attached design drawings. Even if we didn't get the chance to pull it off, at least we can leave our ideas for posterity. If I secured your approval with proposal, then let us try to build this together and submit this to the concept design event. The company and the TGCC will remember it for us. 
I'll be back soon, but I really hope to hear back from you when I reach home, Lonna. 
Love you. 

NOTE: A design draft is attached to this letter. A table listing the manufacturing specifications is provided at the lower right corner of the draft. A temporary name has also been proposed: 
"Junction Point"
added.-4970734592882476450:
"She may come off as cold and heartless, but that is what's needed in the YSTF." A bystander's evaluation of Li Zhiyan is usually very simple. 
All of Li's actions and choices can be boiled down to this statement: Finishing the mission. The troopers of the Yinglung Special Task Force (YSTF) always fight in extreme environments. Cold rationality and sheer determination help guarantee the success of a mission. If "safe exit" is not listed as part of the mission objective, every Yinglung knew what it meant. 
The YSTF has a secret chamber kept under tight security. They do not use it for storing intel files or documents, but for the "belongings" of every Yinglung. When you become an Yinglung, you are given a box and you are free to put anything you want in it. In the event that you fall during a mission, that thing in the box will function as the only proof of your existence. 
But Li Zhiyan's box remained empty. 
She told the chamber custodian that she had nothing. Everything she once had was buried within the dark, cold, and toxic Miasma of the North Wuling Exclusion Zone. 
The custodian had to tell her that this was a YSTF rule. It made Li think. What item best represents herself? If the title of YSTF Captain were taken from her, is there anything that she could put in the box? She pondered about the meaning of "self". 
She was only temporarily hampered by the question. Soon enough, she found an old artist on the market to write her name down with a calligraphy brush. This name was the thing that entered the box. 
The question did not feel important to her. It was the next mission that always mattered most. 
...... 
Li barely visited the secret chamber until recently. In her multiple visits, her once threadbare box began to fill. A pen. A button. A dagger also joined the pile... Every item appeared mundane and meaningless, as though she just grabbed something from the desk. A few seemed like gifts from her neighbors or keepsakes that someone wanted her to pass on... Yet Li treated them all like treasure and carefully placed them in her box. 
When a curious Endministrator asked about this, Li no longer responded with stern silence as she'd often done in the past. Instead, she gave a solemn answer. 
"I looked back and realized that I have missed so many things that I should have treasured."

added.524949210897656216:
[RECEIVING RECORD] 
RECIPIENT: Li Zhiyan 
SENDER: Ling Yue 
SENDER'S ADDRESS: Wuling City 
STATUS: Signed and received 
INSPECTION: Inspection passed 
VOICE MESSAGE: 
Zhiyan! It's me! Auntie Liu! I heard you're getting some R and R at Endfield. You should have told me, Ah Girl. The entire neighborhood got super worried! So I asked the young lady at Serenity Garden to help deliver some of your favorite things! Just a few boxes of osmanthus cakes and sweet kohl pudding. I brewed it just a few days ago for baby's birthday, so it won't keep forever. Finish it first, okay? 
Auntie Shen also cares a lot about you. She stuffed the package with a few sets of clothing that she sewed. She heard you're now staying on a spaceship and so she kept complaining how cold you are in space. So yes, that's where the clothes are from in case you're wondering. 
Ah Girl, I don't know when you'd be back. You've always been stubborn. Always sitting at one spot and doing nothing. You hardly played with other girls your age. Instead, you kept trying to help the aunties in the neighborhood. It's not right! You are a darling girl but we're getting old and won't be around forever. Listen to your Auntie and make some friends at Endfield. I saw their Endmin and the young lady called Perlica, and they look very nice to me! And if you don't know how to make small talk, just take the rice cakes and sweet kohl pudding to the Endfielders, and let them try out your Auntie's cooking! Do this a few times and you'll have new friends! 
Auntie watched you grow up. You never liked to talk, but Auntie knows you got an eye for details. I made a note every time you helped us. Remember when you returned with a photo of Auntie Shen's little girl? Auntie Shen couldn't stop crying that day. Poor girl left when she was around your age, so Auntie Shen treated you like her own. And ... it's the same for all of us. You are a child of Wuling. The city is always your home, no matter where you go. 
Aiyah, the talk is getting too sad. Auntie isn't very good with words, so maybe I should stop here. Just focus on getting better and try to come home early. Once you come back to Wuling, Auntie will make a huge feast for you! Don't forget about Auntie's advice! Bring your new friends too! 
...... 
Wait. Aiyo, Auntie Shen actually has something to say but she started crying again. I'll just let Ling Yue deliver the package and let Auntie Shen record a voice message later. Hope we're not bugging you. Hey! Don't rush me! I'm not taking forever, okay! Wait. Low battery? How — 

[DELIVERY RECORD] 
SENDER: Li Zhiyan 
RECIPIENT: Ling Yue 
SENDER'S ADDRESS: OMV Dijiang 
DELIVERY STATUS: Delivered 
INSPECTION: Inspection passed 
LIST OF ITEM(S): 
Simonch Shawl - 5 pieces 
Breeze of Kjersch - 5 jars 
Cosmic Gate Puzzle - 3 sets 
Citromix [Rf.] - 10 bottles 
VOICE MESSAGE: 
Ling Yue, It's me ... Li Zhiyan. I received the package from the old folks of the neighborhood. Please don't let them deliver another package... There's everything aboard Dijiang, and I still got plenty of stuff left after giving away plenty of things to the Endmin and other Endfielders... 
I think the aunties like the shawls I mailed last time. I procured a few more from the Logistics Division so just let the old ladies sort them out. There are also fruit-flavored mixes you can't find in Wuling and toys for the children. 
Don't worry about the cost. I hardly have any other expenses. 
And tell Auntie Shen that the things are free giveaways from Endfield. I don't want her to worry too much.

added.6589571119810097326: We are thankful for the information you have gathered regarding the locals near Yinglung Pass and their views of the YSTF. Prior to your findings, we knew little regarding the public image of the YSTF. Most of our intel on this subject matter comes from external observations or idle chatter from members of the public. In addition to the documents we have found and the information you have kindly shared, we can see that the residents of Wuling, particularly the survivors from the disaster that took place ten years ago, actively volunteered for Yinglung Pass transport missions and often prepared their own supplies for the YSTF. This shows that the Yinglungs have grassroots support, even though they have been strictly prohibited from accepting any donations from the public.

added.185713362386778433: Almost all of the information that you have uncovered at the Designated Urban Area and Celestial Column was left behind by first-hand witnesses and victims of the disaster from ten years ago. The contents therein describe how the trapped victims attempted to survive and escape the disintegrating Blight Miasma at the early stages of the disaster before full-scale initiation of SAR attempts. Although the Blight Miasma "gradually" washed over the NWEZ, the early onset of the disaster "bisected" the old Science Station. Numerous masses of Miasma then emerged, swallowing up those who could not escape while leaving some trapped in isolated pockets of relative safety. Despite the lack of communications, these survivors did not give up their attempts to escape to safety. The information you found is probably from an old research lab working on solutions for combating the Blight Tide. The lab was once furnished with multiple Xiranite test production facilities, a number of which should still be "active" assuming the absence of other intervening factors. Several early Xiranite experiments focused on the "brute force and quantity" approach. One of the projects for combating the Blight Tide designed Xiranite construction materials used in the Quelltide Levee. The project lab was furnished with substantial Xiranite reserves and various test facilities that people used to help them survive the early stages of the disaster. The information you found also highlighted the decisiveness, perseverance, and professionalism of the Science Station personnel who managed to survive the initial onslaught. They attempted to re-establish communications and organize intellectual assets they could still access.

added.-2793986535455532907:
My combat style? It's based on the arrays that my Father taught me combined with Yinglung tactics. 
Father said that arrays operate on the principle of "change". The array gives the wielder momentum, and moves with the wielder as well. No array should be unchanging or permanent. An array is only complete when the wielder becomes part of it. 
Father brought me to many places when I was a girl. Sometimes we rode logs down rivers. Sometimes we climbed really tall trees. And when we got tired, we'd just lie down on the grass to gaze at the stars... Father taught me their positions and the shapes of the constellations. I later learned that none of this was just a "game". He used a subtle yet engaging way to teach me his knowledge of arrays and the cycles of all sorts of entities. 
The Yinglungs, however, taught me the meaning of "permanence", and how to remain unmoving in the midst of a storm. The Yinglung tactic relies primarily on "momentum" to create an unstoppable force. Different tactics are used against different threats. Yet it does not matter if we fought with swordmancy, spearmancy, or with our formations and arrays. Every Yinglung stuck to their post and focused on unleashing the momentous strike to obliterate the foe. 
I tried integrating Yinglung tactics with my Father's arrays. The former focuses on the team, the entire battlefield, and complementing each other's moves, like how my triplex cannon function as a single unit. My Father's arrays focus on flexibility and change. Combining these two approaches was difficult and troubled me for quite some time... 
But then I revisited the place where Father and I used to play. I saw how falling leaves passed through river rapids, how fowlbeasts perched at the end of long branches swung with the wind, and how the constellations never stopped yet somehow retained their shapes — Then I suddenly understood what Father used to say. I am the key. I must be part of the formation, the fourth piece of the array. 
I am the knot and pivot by which the array changes, the variable element that fills the tactical gap. Once I learned the lesson, I incorporated myself into the array. With my jade slip I positioned the triplex cannon and adjusted its firepower until it felt like an extension of my arm and leg. Only then did I master its use. 
My Originium Arts focus more on the "flow" of energy to create an equilibrium of "change" and "permanence". In a way, my Arts are similar to those wielded by Operator Gilberta. She shared a few tips about Arts casting with me. Operator Arclight also wielded electrical currents with beautiful elegance and mastery, and so I asked her for advice as well. She responded with a phrase: "Feel it." Indeed, Dijiang is a gathering of incredibly talented and skilled individuals and offers plenty of learning opportunities, like Operator Last Rite with her astronomical strength and Operator Pogranichnik with his unparalleled knowledge of military formations... I hope my stay aboard Dijiang will give me a chance to improve my own skills and study the strengths of every school... 
I'm also trying to compile these lessons into a book. Other Yinglungs trying to master the arrays and heavy cannons can use it for reference. I hope these writings can provide them with assistance.

added.-2001610873185504187:
"Age can be a real bummer, old man. Your spearmancy ... got slow and clunky.“ 
The Kuranta thrust the spear into the cracked rooftop and removed the heavy armor pieces. 
"You're just a few years younger than me, so that makes you just as old. Don't tell me that you're still a brat in love with heroic tales." 
"Stop being stupid and head back with me. The warlord Jakub is dead. His dream of 'an even greater city' is no more. This isn't worth dying for." 

"I thought you'd see things my way." the Kuranta looked at the Liberi who stepped over the body of the thirty-ninth Landbreaker, then drew a short blade from a once-ornate scabbard covered in decades of rust. The blade itself glimmered coldly. "I believe in the dream, the one our mentor told us when we were handed these blades. Go back to Terra and let the Golden Age return again. But an Ankhor smashed his tomb. We even couldn't make our way back to the north of Talos-II... Jakub wasn't the only one disappointed. You treat us as dreamers. Tell me then, what is that utopia thing long promised by the Civilization Band when we're facing 'occasional' supply shortages, encroaching Blight masses, and Aggelos attacks?" 

The Liberi sighed with resignation, stopped ten paces away from the Kuranta, pressed a boot into the windswept dust, and made the roof creak. 
"You chose a good place. Strong winds on the roof. The fowlbeasts could easily soar without flapping their wings." 
"Strong winds indeed ... but they're dying down." the Kuranta raised the blade, "We won the Aggeloi War. But what happened next? We're getting old. I'll never live to see my mentor's dream if I kept waiting. I have to act." 
"That's no excuse for following Jakub and pointing our mentor's lance against those who have even less and suffer more." 
"The strong devours the weak. Someone had to do it." the Kuranta straightened and gave a few rather clumsy swings of the blade, "I chose this path to fulfill our mentor's dream... You can't stop me." 
"You were once a hero who charged for ten miles to save a village from an Aggelos attack," the Liberi drew a weapon. It was the exact same sword wielded by the Kuranta. 
He still remembered the day when their mentor handed the blades to them and how his eyes sparkled the moment his fingers touched the weapon. He remembered how his sworn brother asked for their mentor's lance, and how he laughed when his sworn brother boasted about becoming a "great hero". Why did he laugh? The reason escaped his memories. 

"Hero? I'm no hero," the Kuranta raised the blade to chest level, "The hero you mentioned only lives in your mind, or he probably died that night." 
"Your actions disgrace our mentor's lance." the Liberi's handcannon was out of etched ammo and he tossed it away before charging towards the Kuranta, "Return it." 
"See if you're good enough ... to take it off my hands."

<@intru.bold>- Event Rules -</> 
<@gray.note>· You can only challenge stages of this event with teams of up to 2 operators. Complete the challenges to claim generous rewards.</> 
<@gray.note>· You can challenge the stages with your own team (Custom Team) or use a pre-selected team of trained operators (System Team).</>

Input a steady flow rate of <@bl.key>Xiragen</> into a Solid-Gas Transmuting Unit to <@bl.key>activate</> it.
Use the Gasify mode of the Solid-Gas Transmuting Unit to transmute <@bl.key>Cuprium</> into <@bl.key>Cuprium Gas</>.
Input <@bl.key>Cuprium Gas</> and <@bl.key>Separator Cores</> into a Purification Unit switched to Gas Mode to purify and produce <@bl.key>Hetonite Gas</>.

added.6008543993566877738:
Operator Arcane (given name: Li Zhiyan) is a Hongshan Academy of Sciences Yinglung Special Task Force (YSTF) captain. Following injuries suffered during an operation in the North Wuling Exclusion Zone (EZ), she has been permitted to take an extended R&R. With special permission from the YSTF, Arcane is now a partner of Endfield Industries. 

According to Operator Arcane herself, this is her first long R&R. Our medical operators identified several dozen sites of internal injuries. Frontline work in dangerous Blight environments did not destroy her spirit, yet it has taken a toll on her in the form of constant and grueling physical pain. After conversing with members of the HAS, we learned that the HAS hopes that she can use the opportunity for proper recovery. "It should be a break to make up for all the breaks she missed for several years running." 
— Martin Marvin Malen, Assistant, HR Division, Endfield Industries

added.6191673157022112314: Thanks to the data you have uploaded, we can now make the following deductions: The Yinglung Special Task Force (YSTF) is wholly responsible for the defenses of Yinglung Pass. However, the YSTF will require support from other personnel on matters that lie outside their defensive duties. Starting from this conclusion, we perused vast amounts of data and found out that the Hongshan Academy of Sciences (HAS) has, where necessary, authorized short-term residency for laborers or Tianshi who passed their background checks. You may have seen several Tianshi conducting research in this area to handle the threat posed by the Very Large Rift (VLR). Although the North Wuling Exclusion Zone (NWEZ) is more fitting for field research compared to Yinglung Pass, entering the NWEZ would be considered a suicidal act for most people. Yinglungs who periodically venture deep into the NWEZ could not carry many scientific instruments for safety considerations. Hence, the Tianshi were forced to conduct their scientific research in the relative safety of Yinglung Pass. Furthermore, the duration of their residency and the area of their field studies must undergo multiple rounds of review by HAS Headquarters. Any scientific process or task can only be conducted while accompanied by the members of the YSTF.

added.6676613857538089852:
This land might not be anything special. 
Same old plains, same old air, and same old sea. The skies are different, though. There is a huge planet that we could clearly see in the day. There are also no twin moons at night. Life is hardly different from that in Terra. 
Is that a good thing? I don't know. 
I'm here because of national conscription, but It pays and there's food. That's all there's to it. 

But as time went on, I started to feel the differences. 
The land has no Originium. Heck, even the national borders felt like a joke. I thought this was some Columbian frontier pioneering work, where people slogged all day in the wilderness for something to eat. But just today, someone who looked like Leithanian nobility asked me, a worker conscript, about the specifications of machine parts and how to use some machinery. 
And mister nobleman wasn't joking around. 
Ursusian, Leithanian, Columbian... All these divisive labels are now being reduced to crew mate, freund, and pardner. 
I like how things turned out, it's great! But how did it happen? I don't really know. 
I asked around a bit and they're just as confused as I am. So I didn't get my answer for a while until... 

"What is Endfield?" 
I asked the huge Sarkaz team lead this question when I first reported to work. Endfield sounds like a really unlucky name for a company working on the frontiers. 
He did not say anything. Instead, he brought us to the tip of the ship temporarily serving as the Endfield HQ. Told us to have a good look at the planet and the world. 
Planet and world... We hardly used those terms before. 
The team lead saw my confusion and smiled. He told me they are the meaning behind Endfield. 
"Meaning that we gotta use new, pretty words?" 
"Meaning that we left our past on Terra." 

Perhaps he was right. 
Before I decided to walk through the Cosmic Gate to reach this place, I, and everyone else, have forever left something back in Terra. 
People who struggled in the mine shafts, who roamed the wilderness, who worked in the cities, who gave concerts at the spires... Everyone standing shoulder to shoulder aboard the ship, ready to build a future together. 
Fate gave us a choice. We chose something different from the past to create a new way of life. 

What is this world going to be like? don't know. 
Because we're building it right now. 
Buildings will rise from the ground and crops will sprout from the earth. 
This is our resounding answer about the world we're making.

added.7902391980426235494:
To the Respectable Tianshi of Hongshan Academy of Sciences: 
Salutations. 
Though I have pondered long and hard, I do not know to whom this letter should be addressed to. In the end, I asked an acquaintance from your esteemed institution to pen this letter and to attach herein a silk banner (which I have ordered) along with a minor token of my thanks. With great sincerity I hope my gratefulness can be conveyed to the three heroes who saved my life. 
I am a traveling merchant of the TGCC who has spent many years traveling from city to city. Not too long ago, I stopped by a mining town near Alloycarrot to rest and resupply. I did not expect to be ambushed by the Aggeloi that same night. The sudden worsening of the weather only delayed the arrival of a TGCC corporate security detail. The townsfolk and I were forced to hide and protect ourselves. 
I hid in an elevated position inside an abandoned factory building on the edge of the town. The hiding place offered me great vantage to witness everything that happened afterwards. 
From the mass of Blight that suddenly appeared outside the town, three individuals emerged and sauntered into the town. They wore thick, heavy equipment that I had never seen before, yet moved with remarkable speed and agility. I saw faint flickers of light in the darkness accompanied by deep, reverberating roars that reminded me of manglerbeasts. I'm afraid I cannot accurately describe what that was. I only know they wielded extraordinary weapons. The trio then broke formation. One of them charged right into the Aggeloid host, while the other two took the high ground, striking down any Aggelos that accidentally loitered too close to the people. They moved with incredible coordination and tacit understanding, as though they had done the same thing countless times. During my many years as a traveling merchant, I've seen how the Worker's Guard or corporate security fight on multiple occasions. But what I saw that night, especially that individual who went straight to the place where the Aggeloi was the thickest, elevated battle to a whole different level. I can still recall the moves and strikes vividly. It was just like the old Yanese saying. The warrior fought like an "army of one". 
The trio quickly quelled the Aggeloid unrest but we kept hiding until daybreak. A few courageous townsfolk had to sneak out and look around before the rest of us dared to emerge from our hiding places. The threat was eliminated, reduced to rocks and gravel scattered throughout the place. The trio of warriors, however, were gone as well. Although I do not know their name, I am certain they were not fellow townsfolk nor Sarkazian mercenaries as claimed by the townsfolk. From the way they conducted themselves and the equipment they wore, I am certain they hailed from HAS. 
They saved me and everyone living in that mining town. This is an act of charity that we cannot afford to forget. I continued my travels and asked around along the caravan road, but nobody has heard about them. After much deliberation, I am reduced to one final resort. This is why I have sent the letter of appreciation and the silk banner to HAS. I pray the Tianshi can convey our gratitude to the trio who tread upon the Blight. This letter is curt, the credits are meager, and the banner might not look much, but this is the best we can do. 
If fortune smiles on us and this letter is successfully delivered to the intended recipient, please tell them: "We saw what you did that night, and we shall always remember." 
Respectfully Yours. 
A Traveling Merchant. 

"...WHA?! Why did you give this to me? I was just walking by and taught them a few tricks. It's the three of them? No wonder they felt so happy writing up the report. Fine, whatever. I'm busy enough as it is. Handle it your way and stop bothering me! If people hear about this, they'll think I got plenty of time on my hands!"

<@intru.bold>- Participation Requirements -</>
<@gray.note>· After the Event Center is unlocked</>

<@intru.bold>- Event Rules -</>
<@gray.note>· During the event, you can share your exclusive Invite Code to invite [Returning Members] to the event, and earn rewards that include Oroberyls.</>
<@gray.note>· During the event, [Returning Members] can enter and link another player's Invite Code for an exclusive reward of Oroberyls. Once linked, the code cannot be changed.</>
<@gray.note>· Rewards will be sent to the in-game mail of the corresponding account within 10 minutes. The reward mail is valid for 30 days after exchanging the said event rewards.</>
<@gray.note>· The [Returning Member] status and mission completion status within the event may take time to update. Endministrators can try waiting 10 minutes, then refresh and check again.</>

<@intru.bold>- [Returning Member] Details -</>
<@gray.note>· Endministrators who are eligible for the [Protocol Reconnection] event will be considered [Returning Members]. The participation requirements for [Protocol Reconnection] are as follows:</>
<@gray.note>· Endministrator Authority Level ≥ 3</>
<@gray.note>· Complete main mission [Chapter Ⅰ Process Ⅰ: Valley Reboot]</>
<@gray.note>· Not logged into the game for ≥ 21 consecutive days</>
<@gray.note>· Participation in the last [Protocol Reconnection] event ≥ 42 days ago</>
<@gray.note>· During the event, if a player activates the [Returning Member] status multiple times, their event data will carry over from the previous status.</>

added.8599816376172580550:
Happy birthday, Endministrator.

I know I've been around you every day but I still decided to send you this mail. Please don't be too surprised. Thanks to your hard work, Endfield Industries has been doing well and staying on course. Everyone is very grateful, and I'm very glad that I can personally celebrate your birthday. I also hope that I will get to personally celebrate this occasion for the upcoming year and in every year that follows.

Your present this year is a cake. I made every effort to keep the sweetness under control. Please do take a bite.

4094870261298675431.newText: You once taught Perlica many things. The Supervisor tells you about your shared past and her worries. Although you failed to recover your memories, you remain steadfast and never doubted your mission. Like always, you shall overcome all sorts of challenges. You shall continue to travel with Perlica and Endfield Industries and reach the final destination that unites everything as one.

4000466255607108106.oldText: Endmin, how was your holiday break? Plenty of things happened aboard Dijiang and our Medical Division operators kept asking me to remind you to register for your physical examination! Everyone misses you, and I ... I'm also waiting for you, Endmin.
4000466255607108106.newText: Endmin, welcome back. How was your break? Plenty of interesting things have happened on Dijiang while you were away. Medical Division kept saying you haven't had your physical exam, and asked me to remind you to book it. Everyone's been missing you, and I ... I'm so glad you're back, Endministrator.

-1949888752675822153.oldText:
So many monsters... Wait. They are not rocky crawlies! Princess Carly and her Lung hid behind the Stone Forest. There is a powerful Cube-Bot that smashed the squishies with a few shots! Cube-Bot told Princess Carly that the Knights of the Sky visited the place and they even had a nice chat!
"Knights of the Sky, I shall defend the Forest Kingdom, and my will is as pure as the river water!"

8289551009570638203.oldText:
Swings the polearm multiple times to deal 2 hits of <@ba.pd>Physical DMG</>, then slams the ground to deal <@ba.pd>Physical DMG</> and <#ba.knockdown>Knock Down</> to all enemies in the area of effect. 
If the enemy struck by the last hit of this skill has no <#ba.noguard>Vulnerability</> stacks, apply <#ba.physicalvul>Physical Susceptibility</> to the said enemy instead.
8289551009570638203.newText:
Swings the polearm multiple times to deal 2 hits of <@ba.pd>Physical DMG</>, then slams the ground to deal <@ba.pd>Physical DMG</> and <#ba.knockdown>Knock Down</> to all enemies in the area of effect. 
If the enemy struck by the last hit of this skill has no <#ba.noguard>Vulnerability</> stacks, apply <#ba.physicalvul>Physical Susceptibility</> to the said enemy as well.

4876804338743582626.oldText:
<@intru.bold>- Protocol Pass -</> 
Every item on the Protocol Pass is gathered from official operator interviews conducted by the Arsenal Engineers. These items include support projects that everyone hopes to see. 
Activating each seasonal Protocol Pass unlocks the [Basic Supply] tier by default. 
The [Protocol Customized] tier can be purchased, while the [Originium Supply] tier can be unlocked by spending [Origeometry]. 

<@intru.bold>- Protocol Pass EXP and Leveling -</> 
There are 2 major ways to earn Protocol Pass EXP, which then levels up the Protocol Pass and grants you rewards. 
· Complete Pass Missions (some missions will become available after meeting certain criteria). 
· Take part in [Operational Manual - Daily Tasks] and claim the max daily Activity Points rewards. 
※ If max Activity Points is not reached for the day, 1 Vacation Tag will be activated at 04:00 (Server Time) of the following day. Up to 3 tags can be activated. 
※ After earning max Activity Points and claiming the rewards, 1 activated Vacation Tag will be consumed to give double Pass EXP (Daily task rewards are not affected by this). 

Even after maxing the seasonal Protocol Pass level, you can still earn Pass EXP with the aforementioned methods and acquire cyclical rewards. There is no limit to earning Pass EXP that exceed the level cap. 

※ Special EXP bonus details: Unlocking [Originium Supply] / [Protocol Customized] tiers of the seasonal Protocol Pass grants +3% / +3% Pass EXP bonus respectively, for a total of +6% Pass EXP bonus when both tiers are unlocked. 

<@intru.bold>- Protocol Pass Missions Details -</> 
· Weekly Missions: During the Protocol Pass season, weekly missions become available every 7 days. Weekly missions will be reset when the pass season ends. 
· Horizons Missions: These are a special type of Protocol Pass missions that are permanently available. Horizons Missions progress will not be reset when the pass season ends, meaning that these missions can be performed during any pass season to claim their rewards. When all Horizons Missions are completed, its special screen will be closed at 04:00 (Server Time) of the following day. 
 
<@intru.bold>- Protocol Pass Weapons Details -</>
For each pass season, the [Originium Supply] and [Protocol Customized] tiers each contain a special weapon supply.
The weapon supplies and the list of weapons that you can choose from are specific to the pass season. In other words, the list of weapons for weapon supplies from the current season may differ from those of previous seasons. However, once a season weapon supply is received, its list of weapons will not be changed.
[Protocolized Weapon Patterns] can raise the potential of any weapon provided by Protocol Pass weapon supplies, including weapon supplies from future pass seasons. 

<@intru.bold>- Special Rules -</>
When the Protocol Pass season ends, any unclaimed pass level rewards shall be sent to your Mail.
For excessive purchases of [Protocol Customized] due to special circumstances, rewards from [Protocol Customized] tier will not stack. Instead, an amount of [Origeometry] based on the value of the purchase will be returned to you. 

<@intru.bold>- Exchange Crate-o-Surprise Details -</> 
[Exchange Crates-o-Surprise] are automatically opened when acquired. Crate contents are fixed: a random Oroberyl reward and a random progression material reward. 

※ Random Rewards Drop Rates 
· Exchange Crate-o-Surprise [M] 
Oroberyls: Oroberyl×20 (5%) / Oroberyl×15 (35%) / Oroberyl×10 (60%) 
Progression Materials: T-Creds×5000 (15%) / T-Creds×3000 (10%) / Elementary Cognitive Carrier×10 (15%) / Elementary Cognitive Carrier×5 (10%) / Arms INSP Set×2 (15%) / Arms INSP Set×1 (10%) / Protohedron×2 (15%) / Protohedron×1 (10%) 

<@intru.bold>- Cycling Crate-o-Surprise Details -</> 
[Cycling Crates-o-Surprise] come in 2 types: (1) Cycling Crate-o-Surprise [M] and (2) Cycling Crate-o-Surprise [L]. 
[Cycling Crates-o-Surprise] are only acquired from the cycling rewards, and are automatically opened when acquired. Crate contents are fixed: a random Oroberyl reward, a random progression material reward, and a random Sanity Usage Permit reward. 

※ Random Rewards Drop Rates 
· Cycling Crate-o-Surprise [M] 
Oroberyls: Oroberyl×20 (5%) / Oroberyl×15 (35%) / Oroberyl×10 (60%) 
Progression Materials: T-Creds×5000 (15%) / T-Creds×3000 (10%) / Elementary Cognitive Carrier×10 (15%) / Elementary Cognitive Carrier×5 (10%) / Arms INSP Set×2 (15%) / Arms INSP Set×1 (10%) / Protohedron×2 (15%) / Protohedron×1 (10%) 
Sanity Usage Permits: Sanity Usage Permit×1 (90%) / Sanity Usage Permit×2 (10%) 
· Cycling Crate-o-Surprise [L] 
Oroberyls: Oroberyl×30 (5%) / Oroberyl×25 (35%) / Oroberyl×20 (60%) 
Progression Materials: T-Creds×12000 (12.5%) / T-Creds×7500 (12.5%) / Advanced Cognitive Carrier×2 (12.5%) / Advanced Cognitive Carrier×1 (12.5%) / Arms INSP Set×5 (12.5%) / Arms INSP Set×3 (12.5%) / Protohedron×5 (12.5%) / Protohedron×3 (12.5%) 
Sanity Usage Permits: Sanity Usage Permit×3 (90%) / Sanity Usage Permit×4 (10%)

· Total Transfer Value (TTV) is the total value of items that can be moved with a single regional transfer.
· The unit value of every item is marked on its icon.

· Max Number of Items Transferable = TTV / Unit Value of the Item
 
· TTV increases with the regional development level of the source region.
